description: A Japanese street snack of round batter balls cooked in a special molded pan and typically filled with pieces of octopus. They are commonly topped with sauce, mayonnaise, seaweed powder, and bonito flakes.
preparation_steps: Pour batter into the hot takoyaki pan and add octopus and other fillings.
Turn the balls repeatedly as they cook so they become round and evenly browned.
Top with sauce and garnishes before serving hot.

health_advice.benefits: Can provide some seafood protein in small portions.
health_advice.risks: Toppings may add sodium and fat, and the centers stay very hot after cooking.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Eat in moderation and allow them to cool slightly before biting into them.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional takoyaki contains octopus and wheat-based batter. Vegetarian novelty versions exist but are not standard.

allergens: Fish from bonito flakes if used
